title: lupinaster buxbaumii
intro: Lupinaster buxbaumii, also known as the swamp lupin, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Ericaceae. It is native to Europe and Asia and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Lupinaster buxbaumii can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of health conditions, including bronchitis, coughs, and skin irritations.
